---
title: "ChatGPT Promts"
author: "Annick Robatel"
format: html
editor: visual
---

## Here are the promts I used to write to ChatGPT when i got stuck

1.  Is "mep-project-research-4.5." an adequate agent-name to use for the European Parliment open AI?

2.  What exactly does fromJSON(data, flatten = TRUE) do?

3.  I want to save meps_df as csv in data raw now. What is the correct code?

4.  I don't have automatic columns for name and country only the names. Could I write a Loop that gets me directly all the women from the newest parlamentary term (10) and creating new columns for gender and country?

5.  Something does not work. I get Error: parse error: premature EOF (right here) ------\^

6.  I ran: all_women \<- list()\
    \
    for(country in countries){\
    \
    url \<- paste0(\
    "https://data.europarl.europa.eu/api/v2/meps?",\
    "parliamentary-term=10",\
    "&gender=FEMALE",\
    "&country-of-representation=", country,\
    "&limit=500"\
    )\
    \
    response \<- GET(\
    url,\
    add_headers(\
    "User-Agent" = "mep-project-research-4.5.0",\
    "Accept" = "application/ld+json"\
    )\
    )\
    \
    data \<- content(response, as = "text", encoding = "UTF-8")\
    data_json \<- jsonlite::fromJSON(data, flatten = TRUE)\
    \
    df \<- as.data.frame(data_json\$data)\
    \
    df\$country \<- country\
    \
    all_women\[\[country\]\] \<- df\
    }\
    \
    women_df \<- bind_rows(all_women), something still does not work. How could i get it to run?

7.   explain this code line to line to me

    if (status_code(response) != 200) next

    txt \<- content(response, as = "text", encoding = "UTF-8")

    if (nchar(txt) == 0) next

    parsed \<- tryCatch( fromJSON(txt, flatten = TRUE), error = function(e) NULL )

    if (is.null(parsed)) next if (is.null(parsed$data)) next
      if (length(parsed$data) == 0) next

8.  Can I do the same for the men now?

9.  

##
